title: Alcea heldreichii
taxonomy: Alcea heldreichii is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as Heldreich's hollyhock. The classification of Alcea heldreichii is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Malvales, Family: Malvaceae, Genus: Alcea, Species: A. heldreichii. This classification indicates that Alcea heldreichii is a flowering plant that belongs to the mallow family. The plant's classification is closely related to its physical characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Alcea heldreichii, also known as Heldreich's hollyhock or Heldreich's mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae), characterized by its tall, stately stems and showy, pink to purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Alcea heldreichii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Alcea heldreichii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to support its potential health benefits.
